About

Kim Dasha Gustavsson

This space is an archive of my experiments and everyday problem-solving. It is where I test ideas, hit technical roadblocks, and relentlessly figure out how to make them work.

Contact →

What I’m doing now

A 5-phase wearable AI roadmap

I’m working through a step-by-step research project using sensor data, scaling from basic fall detection to predicting panic attacks. Right now, I’m in Phase 1: running an end-to-end ML pipeline with IMU data. I’m testing different form factors (like using my smartphone to collect data) to answer a specific question: Can we accurately detect abnormal movements from a low-stigma placement, like a necklace, rather than a bulky medical device?

Next up: Agentic systems for educators

Looking ahead, I’m planning to build an agentic system designed specifically to help professors and teachers. The goal is to apply my background in informatics and practical AI to create intelligent tools that can genuinely streamline their workflows and reduce administrative friction.

How I work

Identify the real constraint

Before writing any code, I try to figure out the actual bottleneck. Is it a tight deadline, a lack of clean data, or user stigma? I adapt my strategy based on what the project actually needs to succeed.

Momentum over meetings

When a team needs direction, a quick proof-of-concept is usually more effective than a long discussion. I prefer to build a rough prototype and let people interact with it. It aligns everyone faster and keeps the project moving.

Connect the whole pipeline

I like to see the big picture—how raw data eventually becomes a user interface. I enjoy working closely with a team, exchanging feedback, and gradually figuring things out together. The process of solving a problem from end to end is what keeps me engaged.

Build, break, iterate

I expect things to fail on the first try, so I don’t over-plan. Once the main constraint is clear, I move fast. I just build a basic version, see where it breaks, and fix it until it works.

What I work with

AI / ML

Python, PyTorch, scikit-learn. End-to-end ML pipelines, time-series data processing (IMU sensors), edge deployment (ONNX), and practical LLM integrations.

Engineering

TypeScript, React, React Native, and Node.js. Building responsive web interfaces, cross-platform mobile apps, API integrations, and functional full-stack prototypes—backed by databases (SQL/NoSQL) and scalable architectures like Docker and microservices.

Design / Product

Figma, user research, and p5.js (creative coding). Focused on translating complex backend logic and ambiguous user needs into scoped, human-centered interfaces.

Business Insight

Product scoping, market analysis, and aligning technical solutions with business goals. Evaluating feasibility, constraints, and ensuring the software actually delivers real-world value.

Experience

2026 — present MSc in Artificial Intelligence Machine Learning · Deep Learning · NLP · Data Science/Statistics · Cybersecurity · Human-Centred AI
2023 — 2026 BA in Informatics Full-Stack Development, Web Application Architecture, Information Visualization, Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), Data Modeling, Secure Data Handling, User Experience Design

More detail — including earlier work — is on my LinkedIn.
